other_parties.name: THE ROYAL AUTOMOBILE CLUB OF WA INC

merger_description:
Insurance Australia Group (**IAG**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in RAC Insurance (**RACI**) from RACI Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of the Royal Automobile Club of Western Australia Inc (**RAC**) (the **Acquisition**). The Acquisition does not include RAC's roadside assistance business which will continue to be operated independently by RAC.

IAG is a general insurance company operating in Australia and New Zealand. IAG provides a range of personal and commercial insurance products under various brands, including NRMA Insurance, Swann Insurance, ROLLiN’, Cylo, Lumley Special Vehicles and RACQ.

RAC is a Western Australia based member-owned organisation (and, effective from 26 June 2026, a company limited by guarantee) that provides roadside products, general insurance products, and other ancillary services to its members. RAC issues general insurance products to customers through its wholly owned subsidiary RACI under the RAC brand. IAG and RACI are both active in the manufacture and supply of personal lines general insurance, including home insurance and motor insurance products in Australia.

events.title: Decision to Proceed to a Phase 2 review
events.display_title: Decision to Proceed to a Phase 2 review

phase2_notice_matters_to_investigate.heading: Relevant areas of competition
items: The appropriate geographic dimensions of competition, including whether the markets for the supply of motor vehicle insurance and home and contents insurance are national or state-based and whether the markets for the acquisition of smash repair services are regional or state-based.

phase2_notice_matters_to_investigate.heading: Competitive effects in the retail supply of home and contents insurance in Western Australia
items: The various ways and extent to which competition takes place in the supply of home and contents insurance in Western Australia (including price, coverage and other terms of the insurance product offering, customer service, claims handling and renewals).

The extent to which IAG and RACI compete closely in Western Australia, including in relation to price, coverage and other terms of the insurance product offering, customer service, claims handling and renewals.

The competitive significance of RACI in Western Australia, and the likely future competitiveness of RACI absent the Acquisition (taking into account RACI’s position as a state-based organisation, capital adequacy requirements, and RACI’s ability to raise capital).
The competitive constraint from rival insurers on IAG post-Acquisition in Western Australia.
The likelihood, timeliness and sufficiency of new entry into or expansion of the supply of home and contents insurance in Western Australia, including the possible impediments to this.
The impact of the use of different brands (both underwriter and third-party brands) on competition for consumers.
How regulatory costs, claims cost volatility, reinsurance costs and geographic diversity impact RACI’s ability to supply home and contents insurance.
The extent to which RACI’s motor insurance portfolio could mitigate against loss events in RACI’s home and contents insurance portfolio.

phase2_notice_matters_to_investigate.heading: Competitive effects in the retail supply of motor vehicle insurance in Western Australia
items: The various ways and extent to which competition takes place in the supply motor vehicle insurance in Western Australia (including price, coverage and other terms of the insurance product offering, service quality, claims handling and renewals).

The extent to which IAG and RACI compete closely in Western Australia including in relation to price, coverage and other terms of the insurance product offering, customer service, claims handling and renewals.

The competitive significance of RACI in Western Australia, and the likely future competitiveness of RACI absent the Acquisition (taking into account RACI’s position as a state-based organisation, capital adequacy requirements, and RACI’s ability to raise capital).
The competitive constraint from rival insurers on IAG post-Acquisition in Western Australia.
The likelihood, timeliness and sufficiency of new entry into or expansion of the supply of motor insurance in Western Australia, including the possible impediments to this.
The impact of the use of different brands (both underwriter and third-party brands) on competition for consumers.
How regulatory costs, claims cost volatility, reinsurance costs and geographic diversity impact RACI’s ability to supply motor vehicle insurance. Vertical effects
Whether there are regions in Western Australia where smash repair services are limited or capacity constrained.
The degree to which contractual arrangements with smash repairers provide for exclusivity or priority access.
The extent to which smash repairers in practice prioritise work from larger insurers.
The extent to which IAG would be in an enhanced position to foreclose or frustrate competing insurers' access to repairers post-Acquisition.
The likely effect of any foreclosure strategies, including the extent to which rival insurers would become less vigorous competitors in the supply of home and contents insurance and motor insurance.

phase2_notice_matters_to_investigate.heading: Competitive effects in the acquisition of motor vehicle smash repair services
items: Whether smash repairers differ by size, quality, and efficiency in Western Australia and/or certain regions in Western Australia.
The extent to which repair-cost models of insurers influence the prices paid to smash repairers.
The effect of lower repair prices on exit, entry, and investment decisions of smash repairers in Western Australia and/or certain regions in Western Australia.
